basel_iii_formulas.name: Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR)
basel_iii_formulas.formula: LCR = High-Quality Liquid Assets / Total Net Cash Outflows (30 days)
basel_iii_formulas.description: Measures a bank’s ability to withstand a 30-day liquidity stress scenario.

basel_iii_formulas.name: Net Stable Funding Ratio (NSFR)
basel_iii_formulas.formula: NSFR = Available Stable Funding / Required Stable Funding
basel_iii_formulas.description: Ensures long-term funding stability over a one-year horizon.

basel_iii_formulas.name: Leverage Ratio
basel_iii_formulas.formula: Leverage Ratio = Tier 1 Capital / Total Exposure
basel_iii_formulas.description: Limits excessive leverage by comparing core capital to total exposure.

basel_iii_formulas.name: Capital Adequacy Ratio (CAR)
basel_iii_formulas.formula: CAR = (Tier 1 Capital + Tier 2 Capital) / Risk-Weighted Assets
basel_iii_formulas.description: Assesses a bank’s capital strength relative to its risk exposure.

basel_iii_formulas.name: Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) Ratio
basel_iii_formulas.formula: CET1 Ratio = Common Equity Tier 1 Capital / Risk-Weighted Assets
basel_iii_formulas.description: Evaluates the highest quality capital against risk-weighted assets.

basel_iii_formulas.name: Credit Valuation Adjustment (CVA)
basel_iii_formulas.formula: CVA = Market Value of Derivative – CVA Adjustment
basel_iii_formulas.description: Adjusts derivative valuations to reflect counterparty credit risk.

basel_iii_formulas.name: Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR)
basel_iii_formulas.description: Banks must maintain an LCR of at least 100% to ensure they can survive a 30-day liquidity stress scenario.

basel_iii_formulas.name: Net Stable Funding Ratio (NSFR)
basel_iii_formulas.description: Banks must maintain an NSFR of at least 100% to promote long-term funding stability over a one-year horizon.

basel_iii_formulas.name: Leverage Ratio
basel_iii_formulas.description: Tier 1 capital must be at least 3% of total exposure to limit excessive leverage.

basel_iii_formulas.name: Capital Adequacy Ratio (CAR)
basel_iii_formulas.description: Total capital (Tier 1 + Tier 2) must be at least 8% of risk-weighted assets.

basel_iii_formulas.name: Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) Ratio
basel_iii_formulas.description: CET1 capital must be at least 4.5% of risk-weighted assets, excluding buffers.

basel_iii_formulas.name: Capital Conservation Buffer
basel_iii_formulas.description: Additional buffer above minimum capital requirements to absorb losses during periods of financial stress.

basel_iii_formulas.name: Countercyclical Buffer
basel_iii_formulas.description: Variable buffer set by national regulators based on macroeconomic conditions.

basel_iii_legal_requirements.purpose: Ensure short-term liquidity resilience under stress.
Absorb losses during periods of financial stress.
Mitigate systemic risk during credit booms.
Ensure solvency and loss absorption capacity.

basel_iii_legal_requirements.requirement: Capital charge for CVA risk on OTC derivatives
Used to calculate minimum capital requirements

ner_to_user_story_prompts.prompt: As a compliance analyst, I want to monitor the LCR daily so that I can ensure our bank maintains the required 100% threshold during stress scenarios.
As a backend developer, I want to automate NSFR calculations using stable funding data so that we can generate regulatory reports without manual intervention.
As a risk officer, I want to track the capital conservation buffer levels so that we can prevent capital shortfalls during economic downturns.
As a financial engineer, I want to integrate CVA adjustments into our derivative pricing engine so that counterparty risk is accurately reflected.
As a regulatory reporting lead, I want to validate our leverage ratio against Basel III thresholds so that we remain compliant with capital adequacy rules.
As a data scientist, I want to classify assets by risk weight so that we can calculate RWA and optimize capital allocation.
